methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $3M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Drafter is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Chris Barton that has raised $3M+. The valuation exercise references a broader sector peer set of nine private robotics firms and one public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ), though direct stage and valuation-tier peers remain limited.

The Stage-Progression method is applicable and provides the primary valuation signal. Using total capital raised ($3M) as a proxy for current enterprise value and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $7.5M. The Peer-Median approach is not applicable; while eleven robotics and manufacturing companies exist as sector peers, none qualify as narrow comparables when filtered for identical or adjacent stage and similar valuation tier, falling short of the minimum three-datapoint threshold needed for reliable median pricing.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $6M to $9M, centered on $8M, derived from the single applicable method with a ±15% sensitivity band. Key uncertainties include the limited peer comparable set at seed stage in robotics, which constrains cross-check capacity, and the reliance on total-raised-as-proxy methodology absent disclosed recent transaction data. A subsequent financing round or expanded peer universe would materially improve confidence in this range.
